briefdescription.para: Acceleration on the X axis, in m/s^2.
Acceleration on the Y axis, in m/s^2.
Acceleration on the Z axis, in m/s^2.
Acceleration on the X, Y and Z axes.
Angular velocity around the X axis, in rad/s.
Angular velocity around the Y axis, in rad/s.
Angular velocity around the Z axis, in rad/s.
Angular velocity around the X, Y and Z axes.
Magnetic field on the X axis, in G.
Magnetic field on the Y axis, in G.
Magnetic field on the Z axis, in G.
Magnetic field on the X, Y and Z axes.
Device die temperature in degrees Celsius.
Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius.
Pressure in kilopascal.
Proximity.

detaileddescription.para: Adimensional. A value of 1 indicates that an object is close.

briefdescription.para: Humidity, in percent.
Ambient illuminance in visible spectrum, in lux.
Illuminance in visible spectrum, in lux.
Illuminance in infra-red spectrum, in lux.
Illuminance in red spectrum, in lux.
Illuminance in green spectrum, in lux.
Illuminance in blue spectrum, in lux.
Altitude, in meters.
PM1.0 concentration (standard particle, CF=1), in µg/m³
PM2.5 concentration (standard particle, CF=1), in µg/m³
PM10 concentration (standard particle, CF=1), in µg/m³
1.0 micro-meters Particulate Matter, in ug/m^3
2.5 micro-meters Particulate Matter, in ug/m^3
10 micro-meters Particulate Matter, in ug/m^3
Number of particles ≥ 0.3 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Number of particles ≥ 0.5 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Number of particles ≥ 1.0 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Number of particles ≥ 2.5 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Number of particles ≥ 5.0 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Number of particles ≥ 10.0 µm per 0.1 liter of air.
Distance.

detaileddescription.para: From sensor to target, in meters

briefdescription.para: CO2 level, in parts per million (ppm)
O2 level, in parts per million (ppm)
VOC level, in parts per billion (ppb)
Gas sensor resistance in ohms.
Flow rate in litres per minute.
Voltage, in volts.
Current Shunt Voltage in milli-volts.
Current, in amps.
Power in watts.
Resistance , in Ohm.
Angular rotation, in degrees.
Position change on the X axis, in points.
Position change on the Y axis, in points.
Position change on the Z axis, in points.
Position change on the X, Y and Z axis, in points.
Revolutions per minute, in RPM.
Frequency, in Hz.
Voltage, in volts.
Average current, in amps (negative=discharging)
Standby current, in amps (negative=discharging)
Max load current, in amps (negative=discharging)
Gauge temperature
State of charge measurement in %.
Full Charge Capacity in mAh.
Remaining Charge Capacity in mAh.
Nominal Available Capacity in mAh.
Full Available Capacity in mAh.
Average power in mW.
State of health measurement in %.
Time to empty in minutes.
Time to full in minutes.
Cycle count (total number of charge/discharge cycles)
Design voltage of cell in V (max voltage)
Desired voltage of cell in V (nominal voltage)
Desired charging current in mA.
Game Rotation Vector (unit quaternion components X/Y/Z/W)
Gravity Vector (X/Y/Z components in m/s^2)
Gyroscope bias (X/Y/Z components in rad/s)
Raw quadrature decoder count, in counts.
All channels.
Number of all common sensor channels.
This and higher values are sensor specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the sensor header file.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value describing a sensor channel type.
Sensor channels.
Timer-based trigger, useful when the sensor does not have an interrupt line.
Trigger fires whenever new data is ready.
Trigger fires when the selected channel varies significantly.

detaileddescription.para: This includes any-motion detection when the channel is acceleration or gyro. If detection is based on slope between successive channel readings, the slope threshold is configured via the SENSOR_ATTR_SLOPE_TH and SENSOR_ATTR_SLOPE_DUR attributes.

briefdescription.para: Trigger fires when a near/far event is detected.
Trigger fires when channel reading transitions configured thresholds.

detaileddescription.para: The thresholds are configured via the SENSOR_ATTR_LOWER_THRESH, SENSOR_ATTR_UPPER_THRESH, and SENSOR_ATTR_HYSTERESIS attributes.

briefdescription.para: Trigger fires when a single tap is detected.
Trigger fires when a double tap is detected.
Trigger fires when a free fall is detected.
Trigger fires when motion is detected.
Trigger fires when no motion has been detected for a while.
Trigger fires when the FIFO watermark has been reached.
Trigger fires when the FIFO becomes full.
Trigger fires when a tilt is detected.
Trigger fires when data overflows.
Number of all common sensor triggers.
This and higher values are sensor specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the sensor header file.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value describing a sensor trigger type.
Sensor trigger types.
Sensor sampling frequency, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: how many times a second the sensor takes a measurement.

briefdescription.para: Lower threshold for trigger.
Upper threshold for trigger.
Threshold for any-motion (slope) trigger.
Duration for which the slope values needs to be outside the threshold for the trigger to fire.
Oversampling factor.
Sensor range, in SI units.
The sensor value returned will be altered by the amount indicated by offset: final_value = sensor_value + offset.
Calibration target.

detaileddescription.para: This will be used by the internal chip's algorithms to calibrate itself on a certain axis, or all of them.

briefdescription.para: Configure the operating modes of a sensor.
Set a calibration value needed by a sensor.
Enable/disable sensor features.
Alert threshold or alert enable/disable.
Free-fall duration represented in milliseconds.

detaileddescription.para: If the sampling frequency is changed during runtime, this attribute should be set to adjust freefall duration to the new sampling frequency.

briefdescription.para: Hardware batch duration in ticks.
Configure the gain of a sensor.
Configure the resolution of a sensor.
Chip ID of a sensor.
Number of all common sensor attributes.
This and higher values are sensor specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the sensor header file.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value describing a sensor attribute type.
Sensor attribute types.
Include whatever data is associated with the trigger.
Do nothing with the associated trigger data, it may be consumed later.
Flush/clear whatever data is associated with the trigger.
Options for what to do with the associated data when a trigger is consumed.
Callback API upon firing of a trigger.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The trigger

briefdescription.para: Callback function used with the helper processing function.

parameterdescription.para: The result code of the read (0 being success)
The data buffer holding the sensor data
The length (in bytes) of the buf
The optional userdata passed to sensor_read_async_mempool()

briefdescription.para: Check if channel specs are equivalent.

parameterdescription.para: First chan spec
Second chan spec
If equivalent
If not equivalent

briefdescription.para: Decode N frames using a sensor_decode_context.

parameterdescription.para: The context to use for decoding
The output buffer
Maximum number of frames to decode

simplesect.para: The decode result from sensor_decoder_api's decode function

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum sensor_channel chan, enum sensor_attribute attr, const struct sensor_value *val)
memberdef.name: sensor_attr_set

briefdescription.para: Set an attribute for a sensor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The channel the attribute belongs to, if any. Some attributes may only be set for all channels of a device, depending on device capabilities.
The attribute to set
The value to set the attribute to

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum sensor_channel chan, enum sensor_attribute attr, struct sensor_value *val)
memberdef.name: sensor_attr_get

briefdescription.para: Get an attribute for a sensor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The channel the attribute belongs to, if any. Some attributes may only be set for all channels of a device, depending on device capabilities.
The attribute to get
Pointer to where to store the attribute

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct sensor_trigger *trig, sensor_trigger_handler_t handler)
memberdef.name: sensor_trigger_set

briefdescription.para: Activate a sensor's trigger and set the trigger handler.

detaileddescription.para: The handler will be called from a thread, so I2C or SPI operations are safe. However, the thread's stack is limited and defined by the driver. It is currently up to the caller to ensure that the handler does not overflow the stack.

The user-allocated trigger will be stored by the driver as a pointer, rather than a copy, and passed back to the handler. This enables the handler to use CONTAINER_OF to retrieve a context pointer when the trigger is embedded in a larger struct and requires that the trigger is not allocated on the stack.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The trigger to activate
The function that should be called when the trigger fires

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Fetch a sample from the sensor and store it in an internal driver buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Read all of a sensor's active channels and, if necessary, perform any additional operations necessary to make the values useful. The user may then get individual channel values by calling sensor_channel_get.
The function blocks until the fetch operation is complete.
Since the function communicates with the sensor device, it is unsafe to call it in an ISR if the device is connected via I2C or SPI.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Fetch a sample from the sensor and store it in an internal driver buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Read and compute compensation for one type of sensor data (magnetometer, accelerometer, etc). The user may then get individual channel values by calling sensor_channel_get.
This is mostly implemented by multi function devices enabling reading at different sampling rates.
The function blocks until the fetch operation is complete.
Since the function communicates with the sensor device, it is unsafe to call it in an ISR if the device is connected via I2C or SPI.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The channel that needs updated

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum sensor_channel chan, struct sensor_value *val)
memberdef.name: sensor_channel_get

briefdescription.para: Get a reading from a sensor device.

detaileddescription.para: Return a useful value for a particular channel, from the driver's internal data. Before calling this function, a sample must be obtained by calling sensor_sample_fetch or sensor_sample_fetch_chan. It is guaranteed that two subsequent calls of this function for the same channels will yield the same value, if sensor_sample_fetch or sensor_sample_fetch_chan has not been called in the meantime.

For vectorial data samples you can request all axes in just one call by passing the specific channel with _XYZ suffix. The sample will be returned at val[0], val[1] and val[2] (X, Y and Z in that order).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sensor device
The channel to read
Where to store the value

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the sensor's decoder API.

parameterdescription.para: The sensor device
Pointer to the decoder which will be set upon success

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, const struct device *sensor, const struct sensor_chan_spec *channels, size_t num_channels)
memberdef.name: sensor_reconfigure_read_iodev

briefdescription.para: Reconfigure a reading iodev.

detaileddescription.para: Allows a reconfiguration of the iodev associated with reading a sample from a sensor.

Important: If the iodev is currently servicing a read operation, the data will likely be invalid. Please be sure the flush or wait for all pending operations to complete before using the data after a configuration change.
It is also important that the data field of the iodev is a sensor_read_config.

parameterdescription.para: The iodev to reconfigure
The sensor to read from
One or more channels to read
The number of channels in channels

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, struct rtio *ctx, void *userdata, struct rtio_sqe **handle)
memberdef.name: sensor_stream

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, struct rtio *ctx, uint8_t *buf, size_t buf_len)
memberdef.name: sensor_read

briefdescription.para: Blocking one shot read of samples from a sensor into a buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Using cfg, read data from the device by using the provided RTIO context ctx. This call will generate a rtio_sqe that will be given the provided buffer. The call will wait for the read to complete before returning to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: The iodev created by SENSOR_DT_READ_IODEV
The RTIO context to service the read
Pointer to memory to read sample data into
Size in bytes of the given memory that are valid to read into

briefdescription.para: One shot non-blocking read with pool allocated buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Using cfg, read one snapshot of data from the device by using the provided RTIO context ctx. This call will generate a rtio_sqe that will leverage the RTIO's internal mempool when the time comes to service the read.

parameterdescription.para: The iodev created by SENSOR_DT_READ_IODEV
The RTIO context to service the read
Optional userdata that will be available when the read is complete

briefdescription.para: Helper function for common processing of sensor data.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be called in a blocking manner after sensor_read() or in a standalone thread dedicated to processing. It will wait for a cqe from the RTIO context, once received, it will decode the userdata and call the cb. Once the cb returns, the buffer will be released back into ctx's mempool if available.

parameterdescription.para: The RTIO context to wait on
Callback to call when data is ready for processing

briefdescription.para: Helper function to convert acceleration from m/s^2 to Gs.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct holding the acceleration, in m/s^2.

briefdescription.para: Helper function to convert acceleration from Gs to m/s^2.

parameterdescription.para: The G value to be converted.
A pointer to a sensor_value struct, where the result is stored.

briefdescription.para: Helper function to convert acceleration from m/s^2 to milli Gs.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct holding the acceleration, in m/s^2.

simplesect.para: The converted value, in milli Gs.

briefdescription.para: Helper function to convert acceleration from m/s^2 to micro Gs.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct holding the acceleration, in m/s^2.

simplesect.para: The converted value, in micro Gs.

briefdescription.para: Helper function to convert acceleration from micro Gs to m/s^2.

parameterdescription.para: The micro G value to be converted.
A pointer to a sensor_value struct, where the result is stored.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting radians to degrees.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct, holding the value in radians.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting degrees to radians.

parameterdescription.para: The value (in degrees) to be converted.
A pointer to a sensor_value struct, where the result is stored.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting radians to 10 micro degrees.

detaileddescription.para: When the unit is 1 micro degree, the range that the int32_t can represent is +/-2147.483 degrees. In order to increase this range, here we use 10 micro degrees as the unit.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct, holding the value in radians.

simplesect.para: The converted value, in 10 micro degrees.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting 10 micro degrees to radians.

parameterdescription.para: The value (in 10 micro degrees) to be converted.
A pointer to a sensor_value struct, where the result is stored.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to double.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to float.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting double to struct sensor_value.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.
The converted value.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting float to struct sensor_value.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.
The converted value.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to integer deci units.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to integer centi units.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to integer milli units.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting struct sensor_value to integer micro units.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting integer milli units to struct sensor_value.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.
The converted value.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for converting integer micro units to struct sensor_value.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a sensor_value struct.
The converted value.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a sensor_decode_context.

memberdef.name: SENSOR_DT_READ_IODEV
memberdef.initializer: static struct sensor_chan_spec _CONCAT(__channel_array_, name)[] = {__VA_ARGS__}; \ static struct sensor_read_config _CONCAT(__sensor_read_config_, name) = { \ .sensor = DEVICE_DT_GET(dt_node), \ .is_streaming = false, \ .channels = _CONCAT(__channel_array_, name), \ .count = ARRAY_SIZE(_CONCAT(__channel_array_, name)), \ .max = ARRAY_SIZE(_CONCAT(__channel_array_, name)), \ }; \ RTIO_IODEV_DEFINE(name, &__sensor_iodev_api, _CONCAT(&__sensor_read_config_, name))

briefdescription.para: Define a reading instance of a sensor.

detaileddescription.para: Use this macro to generate a rtio_iodev for reading specific channels. Example:

memberdef.name: SENSOR_DT_STREAM_IODEV
memberdef.initializer: static struct sensor_stream_trigger _CONCAT(__trigger_array_, name)[] = {__VA_ARGS__}; \ static struct sensor_read_config _CONCAT(__sensor_read_config_, name) = { \ .sensor = DEVICE_DT_GET(dt_node), \ .is_streaming = true, \ .triggers = _CONCAT(__trigger_array_, name), \ .count = ARRAY_SIZE(_CONCAT(__trigger_array_, name)), \ .max = ARRAY_SIZE(_CONCAT(__trigger_array_, name)), \ }; \ RTIO_IODEV_DEFINE(name, &__sensor_iodev_api, &_CONCAT(__sensor_read_config_, name))

briefdescription.para: Define a stream instance of a sensor.

detaileddescription.para: Use this macro to generate a rtio_iodev for starting a stream that's triggered by specific interrupts. Example:

briefdescription.para: checks if a given channel is a 3-axis channel

parameterdescription.para: The channel to check
if chan is any of SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_XYZ, SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_XYZ, or SENSOR_CHAN_MAGN_XYZ, or SENSOR_CHAN_POS_DXYZ
otherwise

briefdescription.para: checks if a given channel is an Accelerometer

parameterdescription.para: The channel to check
if chan is any of SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_XYZ, SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_X, or SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_Y, or SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_Z
otherwise

briefdescription.para: checks if a given channel is a Gyroscope

parameterdescription.para: The channel to check
if chan is any of SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_XYZ, SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_X, or SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_Y, or SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_Z
otherwise

briefdescription.para: The value of gravitational constant in micro m/s^2.
The value of constant PI in micros.

memberdef.name: SENSOR_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: DEVICE_DT_DEFINE(node_id, init_fn, pm_device, \ data_ptr, cfg_ptr, level, prio, \ api_ptr, __VA_ARGS__); \ \ SENSOR_INFO_DT_DEFINE(node_id);

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() with sensor specifics.

detaileddescription.para: Defines a device which implements the sensor API. May define an element in the sensor info iterable section used to enumerate all sensor devices.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Name of the init function of the driver.
PM device resources reference (NULL if device does not use PM).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Priority within the selected initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Like SENSOR_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to SENSOR_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().
other parameters as expected by SENSOR_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for sensors.
